LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE COURT TOOK UNDER SUBMISSION DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL REQUEST FOR RULING ON THE EVIDENTARY OBJECTIONS. DR. SIMON'S DECLARATION FAILS TO ADDRESS AN ESSENTIAL ELEMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S CASE, I.E. THAT DEFEFNDANTS' FAILURE TO MEET THE STANDARD OF CARE CAUSED PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES. COUNSEL WILL PREPARE OBJECTIONS AND SUBMIT THEM TO THE COURT FOR REVIEW. DEFENDANT ST LUKES HOSPITAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion Compelling Pltf'S 1. Responses To Form Interrogatories(Set One); Custom Interrogatories (Set One); Request For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Request For Admissions And 2. Production Of All Responsive Documents; 3. To Establish Admissions And 4. For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
(Telephonic Appearance) Tascha C. Haut for defendant and moving party: Shadi Shakeri and Roham Zamanian, M.D.
William Campisi, Jr. for plaintiff and opposing party: Sheila J. Cohen

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Shadi Shakeri's motion to compel responses to form and special interrogatories and request for production of documents (set #1) and to deem certain matters admitted.
The Court has read and considered plaintiff's late-filed opposition. Defendant's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories is moot as responses have been served. The Court makes no rulings as to the sufficiency of the responses. The motion to compel responses to special interrogatories and request for production of documents (set #1) is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objections within 15 days of notice of this order. The motion to deem certain matters admitted is denied as responses have been served. The Court finds that plaintiff failed to respond in a timely fashion and to make a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the matters in dispute thereby requiring the filing of the instant motion. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $680 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Mo To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Purs To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 1985.3 & 1987.1
Off Calendar Dec-22-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: KEVIN R. MINTZ, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANTS/MOVING PARTIES, CARL MANI, SANGEETA GAMBHIR, M.D., STUART MANSFIELD, M.D.
KRISTEN A. PICO, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY, ST. LUKE'S HOSPITAL
WILLIAM CAMPISI, JR., ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF, SHEILA J. COHEN

MATTER ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION. Defendant's motion to compel compliance with deposition subpoena to St. Luke's Hospital is granted. The Court has read and considered the late-filed opposition by plaintiff. The deponent is ordered to comply with the subpoena within 10 days of notice of this order. MODIFICATION: COMPLIANCE DATE OF JANUARY 6, 2006. (610/BEC)
